steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage sets up a contrast: the sculpture endures harsh environmental exposure, yet remains in good condition. The transition must acknowledge the first fact (concede it) while pivoting to the surprising second fact. A word or phrase of concession — not causation or simple addition — is needed.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As a result,: implies the harsh conditions caused the good preservation, which reverses logic. ✗
  • Choice B — That said,: signals concession — it grants the harsh exposure just mentioned, then pivots to the contrasting fact that the sculpture is preserved. ✓
  • Choice C — In addition,: merely adds information without signaling the contrast the passage requires. ✗
  • Choice D — On the contrary,: overstates the opposition; the passage presents a nuanced contrast, not a flat contradiction. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
